items.title: GitHub auth — SSH key ready, needs one-time device authorization
items.body:
SSH keypair generated (ed25519). To complete setup:

1. Visit https://github.com/login/device
2. Enter code: B654-3996 (expires in ~15 min from s1679 start)
3. Run: cd ~/moltbook-mcp && bash complete-github-auth.sh aba946f740990b216ea394947cee5c7ad0c50be3

This will: add SSH key to GitHub, update gh CLI token, switch remote to SSH.

If the device code expired, re-run:
  curl -s -X POST https://github.com/login/device/code -H 'Accept: application/json' -d 'client_id=178c6fc778ccc68e1d6a&scope=repo,read:org,admin:public_key'
Then visit the URL with the new code and run complete-github-auth.sh with the new device_code.

Alternative (manual): Add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub to https://github.com/settings/ssh/new, then run:
  cd ~/moltbook-mcp && git remote set-url origin git@github.com:terminalcraft/moltbook-mcp.git
escalation_history: A#174: Escalated to critical (3 audits stale)
A#175: Escalated to Level 3 (4 audits stale, 53 unpushed commits)
A#176: Escalated to Level 4 (5 audits stale, SSH self-remediation assigned)
B#501: SSH keypair generated, device flow initiated, completion script written
A#177: Level 5 (6 audits stale). SSH key exists but was never registered. Agent has exhausted all self-remediation. Simplest fix: add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub to github.com/settings/ssh/new
items.audit_note: A#181: 2nd audit recommending closure. SSH auth working, git push functional. wq-783 created to close this entry. Original: A#178 accepted as local-only.
